summary: Miscellaneous game-mode decomp work centered on gm_1832, All-Star, Trophy Fall, and shared HSD_SObj/JObj typing. The PR fully matched gm_1832 functions fn_801859C8 and fn_80185D64, made gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter fully match, substantially implemented gm_801B60A4_OnLoad, and improved gmregtyfall matching by replacing verbose null-safe child traversal and inline-expanded JObj setter code with matching helper wrappers/static-header definitions. It also cleaned up baselib SObj naming from HSD_SObj_803A477C_t to HSD_SObj and propagated more accurate HSD_JObj return types through ftLib_800865F0 and Player_80036EA0.
what_changed: Implemented gm_1832 function fn_801859C8 as an HSD_SObj list walker that decrements sobj->x48 timers and clears bit 0 of sobj->x40 when the timer reaches zero.

Implemented gm_1832 function fn_80185D64, calling ftDemo_ObjAllocInit and Player_InitAllPlayers, then iterating left/right character id data in lbl_804735E8 to load player resources via Player_80036E20, including CKIND_GKOOPS to CKIND_KOOPA normalization.
Updated gm_1832.h prototypes for fn_801859C8 and fn_80185D64 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void signatures.
Added partial semantic comments to lbl_804735E8 fields in gm_1832.c, including model scale, game type, IrRdMap progress, left/right character counts, and left/right character id arrays.
Added static HSD_Archive* lbl_804D65F4 in gm_1832.c.

Implemented most of gm_801B60A4_OnLoad in gmallstar.c: initializes All-Star data, clears gm_80490940 as 25 bytes, randomizes gm_803DEBE8 entries with HSD_Randi, initializes UnkAllstarData callbacks/arrays, sets scene minor 0x70, calls gm_80172174, and calls Ground_801C5A28.
Added gmallstar.static.h with gm_80490940_t and gm_803DEBE8_t struct definitions plus externs for gm_803DEBE8[25] and gm_80490940[5].

Expanded struct UnkAllstarData in gm/types.h from padding-heavy placeholders into named offsets for callback/function-pointer-looking fields, current percent, character id array x76[24], x90[4], and current time x9C.
Refined gmregtyfall.c types by changing gm_804D6798 from UNK_T to HSD_Joint* and Player_80036EA0 result handling from integer-like values to HSD_JObj*.
Simplified gmregtyfall OnEnter child traversal by using HSD_JObjGetChild and HSD_JObjGetNext instead of repeated manual null-check child/next chains.

Added gmregtyfall.static.h with fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z, fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetRotationY, fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z, fake_HSD_ASSERT, and ftCo_800C6AFC wrapper layers to reproduce matching inline/assert behavior.
Changed ftLib_800865F0 return type from void* to HSD_JObj* in both ftlib.c and ftlib.h.
Changed Player_80036EA0 return type from s32 to HSD_JObj* in player.c/player.h and returned NULL instead of integer 0 when no entity exists.
Renamed/normalized HSD_SObj_803A477C_t to HSD_SObj in baselib forward declarations and sobjlib.h, adding an explicit next pointer, widening x40 to u32, and adding x48.
Propagated HSD_SObj* type usage through gm movie/opening/omake/title-related code, soundtest, toy, lbmthp, and gmregtyfall.
Removed the local lbmthp static extended HSD_SObj_803A477C_t_ext stand-in because sobjlib.h now exposes the needed HSD_SObj fields.
Used PAD_STACK macros in places where anonymous stack padding was previously expressed as local byte arrays, such as gmMainLib_8015D984.
smart_moves: Promoting ftLib_800865F0 and Player_80036EA0 to return HSD_JObj* removed integer casts and let gmregtyfall express JObj relationships directly.
Centralizing the SObj layout as struct HSD_SObj avoided one-off extended local structs in lbmthp and made next/x40/x48 accesses reusable for gm_1832 and other UI/movie code.

The gmregtyfall static header documents that the fake_tyfall JObj setters are matching aids for an inline issue rather than presumed real engine APIs, which preserves evidence boundaries while improving the match.
Using HSD_JObjGetChild/HSD_JObjGetNext compacted long compiler-artifact-style child traversal while preserving the nested access pattern needed for matching.
The All-Star static header isolated file-local data layout guesses for gm_803DEBE8 and gm_80490940, making gm_801B60A4_OnLoad easier to read without prematurely over-naming every field.
Adding offset comments and limited semantic notes to UnkAllstarData and lbl_804735E8 improves future struct recovery while keeping uncertain names in xNN form.

The gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter change kept the return value from gmTitle_801A1AC0 in a local HSD_Archive* and cast it unused, replacing stack padding and achieving the last byte needed for a full match.
decomp_lessons: When a function returns a known engine object, propagate the precise pointer type through all callers instead of preserving void* or s32 placeholders; this can eliminate casts and unlock cleaner downstream matches.
If multiple files use the same partially known engine struct, prefer improving the shared baselib header over duplicating local extended structs.
Static headers are useful for file-local matching scaffolding, especially for data symbols, inline-sensitive helpers, and comments about unresolved compiler behavior.

For HSD_JObj setter inlines, matching may require reproducing original assert line numbers and invalidation calls; wrapper layers can control inlining when the shared inline behavior does not match in a specific TU.
Replacing verbose null-safe manual child traversal with known helper accessors can improve readability and still match, but this depends on the helper's exact inline/codegen behavior.
Array layout recovery in game-mode structs can be driven by loop indexing patterns; here All-Star and 1P splash data became clearer through character-count and character-id iteration.
For partial data structs, offset-preserving xNN names plus comments are safer than overconfident semantic names.
naming_conventions: Canonicalized HSD_SObj_803A477C_t to HSD_SObj in baselib and all observed users.
Used HSD_JObj* and HSD_Joint* for object/joint pointers instead of void*, UNK_T, or s32.
Kept uncertain globals and fields in existing decomp style such as lbl_804735E8, gm_80473A18, gm_803DEBE8_t, gm_80490940_t, and xNN fields.
Named file-local matching helpers with an explicit fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* prefix to signal they are not necessarily canonical baselib APIs.
Used .static.h files for file-local structs, extern data declarations, and matching-only inline helpers: gmallstar.static.h and gmregtyfall.static.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK instead of anonymous local byte arrays to preserve stack layout in matching functions.
Introduced local temporary archive variable plus `(void) archive` in gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter to replace PAD_STACK and finish the function match.
Created fake HSD_JObj setter inlines with fake_HSD_ASSERT line numbers matching jobj.h assertions and calls through ftCo_800C6AFC_dontinline wrappers to manage inline/codegen behavior.
Preserved All-Star initialization code shape with manual byte clearing of gm_80490940 and explicit repeated writes to x76 entries, likely because loop or memset forms affected matching.
Used a local struct tmp and pointer walking for gm_803DEBE8 randomization/shuffle in gm_801B60A4_OnLoad.
In fn_80185D64, retained pointer arithmetic with comments noting it is probably an inner-struct issue because the code currently needs ptr[0x0D] and ptr[0x10] accesses from &lbl_804735E8.xE4.
The decomp.dev report measured +1304 matched bytes, three new full matches, and three improvements in unmatched items.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 52.21% (+0.03%, +1304 bytes).
The bot listed three new matches: gm_1832 fn_80185D64 (+208 bytes), gm_1832 fn_801859C8 (+68 bytes), and gmtitle gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter (+1 byte).
The bot listed improvements for gmallstar gm_801B60A4_OnLoad from 0.00% to 87.40%, soundtest un_80301964 from 96.25% to 98.12%, and gmregtyfall gm_801A7070_OnEnter from 98.85% to 99.03%.
The bot also reported one unmatched-item regression in gm_1832 .sbss from 44.44% to 43.75%; the slice does not explain whether this was accepted intentionally.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that replace fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* helpers with canonical HSD_JObj setters or resolve the ftCo_800C6AFC inline issue.
Check whether HSD_SObj layout fields x40 and x48 were later named or expanded beyond the minimal layout introduced here.
Investigate later gm_1832 work for a proper inner struct around lbl_804735E8.xE4/xF1/xF4 to remove pointer arithmetic in fn_80185D64.
Search for later All-Star PRs that finish gm_801B60A4_OnLoad after this PR reached 87.40%.
Search for later changes to UnkAllstarData x48/x4C/x50/x54/x64/x68 callback-like fields and x76 character id array naming.
Check whether the gm_1832 .sbss regression noted by decomp.dev was later corrected.
